name: Kotlin
description: Modern statically-typed language for Android; server; and multiplatform development.
when_to_use: Default for all new Android development. Required for Jetpack Compose.
trade_offs: Kotlin-specific patterns (coroutines; sealed classes; extensions) have learning curve from Java.
implementation_notes: Android: Kotlin 2.1+ with K2 compiler. Built-in Kotlin in AGP 9.x (no plugin needed). KMM: shared modules.
keywords: kotlin android language k2 compiler coroutines sealed

name: Swift
description: Apple's modern programming language for iOS; macOS; watchOS; and tvOS.
when_to_use: Default for all new Apple platform development. Required for SwiftUI.
trade_offs: Swift-only frameworks exclude Objective-C interop in some cases. ABI stability since Swift 5.
implementation_notes: iOS: Swift 5.10+. Concurrency: async/await + actors. UI: SwiftUI or UIKit. Package manager: SPM.
keywords: swift ios language concurrency actor async await spm

name: Java
description: Established JVM language with extensive Android ecosystem and library support.
when_to_use: Legacy Android projects; interop with existing Java libraries; enterprise backends.
trade_offs: Verbose compared to Kotlin; no coroutines; limited functional programming support.
implementation_notes: Android: Java 21+ (supported in AGP 9.x). Gradle: Java or Kotlin DSL. Still supported but Kotlin preferred.
keywords: java android jvm legacy enterprise interop

name: Objective-C
description: Apple's legacy programming language still used in many iOS codebases.
when_to_use: Maintaining legacy iOS projects; using C/C++ libraries with direct interop.
trade_offs: Dynamic typing; manual memory management patterns; shrinking community. Mix with Swift using bridging header.
implementation_notes: iOS: Objective-C with ARC. Interop: Swift bridging header. Still in UIKit internals.
keywords: objective-c objc ios legacy bridging arc

name: Dart
description: Google's language powering Flutter for cross-platform mobile development.
when_to_use: When building Flutter apps for iOS; Android; web; and desktop from one codebase.
trade_offs: Smaller ecosystem than Kotlin/Swift; hot reload is a major productivity advantage.
implementation_notes: Flutter: Dart 3.3+ with sound null safety. Package manager: pub. Build: flutter build.
keywords: dart flutter language null safety hot reload pub

name: Jetpack Compose
description: Modern declarative UI toolkit for Android replacing XML layouts.
when_to_use: All new Android UI development. Existing apps migrating from View system.
trade_offs: Compose compiler stability; some missing View equivalents; learning curve for imperative devs.
implementation_notes: Android: Compose BOM 2024.x+. Material 3 components. Compose Navigation.
keywords: jetpack compose declarative ui android material3 bom

name: SwiftUI
description: Apple's declarative UI framework for all Apple platforms.
when_to_use: New iOS 17+ projects or screens. Modernizing UIKit apps incrementally.
trade_offs: Limited backward compatibility; some UIKit features not yet available in SwiftUI.
implementation_notes: iOS: SwiftUI with @Observable (iOS 17+) or Observation. NavigationStack. Charts framework.
keywords: swiftui declarative ui ios observable navigation

name: UIKit
description: Imperative UI framework for iOS with full platform API access.
when_to_use: Legacy iOS projects; complex custom UI; or features not yet in SwiftUI.
trade_offs: Verbose; storyboard merge conflicts; manual layout code (SnapKit or NSLayoutConstraint).
implementation_notes: iOS: UIKit with programmatic layout or Storyboards. UIHostingController for Compose integration.
keywords: uikit imperative ios storyboard layout constraint

name: Flutter
description: Google's cross-platform UI toolkit using Dart and Skia rendering engine.
when_to_use: When maximum code sharing across iOS; Android; web; and desktop is needed.
trade_offs: Custom rendering means no native look-and-feel; platform plugin quality varies.
implementation_notes: Flutter: Dart 3.3 + Material 3 or Cupertino widgets. State: BLoC; Riverpod; or Provider.
keywords: flutter cross platform dart skia material cupertino

name: Kotlin Multiplatform (KMM)
description: Share Kotlin business logic between Android and iOS with native UI.
when_to_use: When you want shared domain/data layers but native UI per platform.
trade_offs: iOS interop requires SKIE or manual wrappers; Swift/Kotlin bridging complexity.
implementation_notes: KMM: shared module (commonMain/androidMain/iosMain). Networking: Ktor. DB: SQLDelight. DI: Koin.
keywords: kotlin multiplatform kmm kmp shared skie ktor

name: Compose Multiplatform
description: JetBrains framework extending Compose to iOS; desktop; and web.
when_to_use: When wanting maximum code sharing including UI layer with Compose-style development.
trade_offs: iOS support is production-ready but younger than Flutter; desktop support is strong.
implementation_notes: CMP: shared @Composable UI. Platform: expect/actual. Resources: Compose resources API.
keywords: compose multiplatform cmp jetbrains ios desktop web

name: Room (Android)
description: SQLite abstraction library with compile-time query verification for Android.
when_to_use: Default local database for Android apps. Works with Flow and coroutines.
trade_offs: Android-only; for KMM use SQLDelight instead. Migration management required.
implementation_notes: Room: @Entity + @Dao + @Database. Migrations: autoMigrate or manual Migration class. Use Flow for reactive queries.
keywords: room database sqlite android dao entity migration

name: Core Data (iOS)
description: Apple's object graph and persistence framework for iOS/macOS.
when_to_use: When building iOS-only apps needing complex object relationships and iCloud sync.
trade_offs: Complex API; thread safety requires NSManagedObjectContext per thread. Heavy for simple key-value.
implementation_notes: iOS: NSManagedObjectContext + NSFetchRequest. Use @FetchRequest in SwiftUI. NSPersistentCloudKitContainer for sync.
keywords: coredata database ios persistence icloud nsfetchrequest

name: SQLDelight (Multiplatform)
description: Kotlin Multiplatform SQL database with type-safe generated Kotlin APIs.
when_to_use: When using KMM and need shared local database across Android and iOS.
trade_offs: Requires writing raw SQL (not ORM). Generated code needs rebuild on schema change.
implementation_notes: SQLDelight: .sq files generate Kotlin interfaces. Drivers: Android SQLite; iOS Native. Coroutines Flow adapter.
keywords: sqldelight database multiplatform sql kotlin kmp

name: Retrofit (Android)
description: Type-safe HTTP client for Android with converter and interceptor support.
when_to_use: Default networking library for Android-only or Android-focused projects.
trade_offs: Android-only; for KMM use Ktor. OkHttp dependency adds to APK size.
implementation_notes: Retrofit: @GET/@POST annotations + Gson/Moshi converter. OkHttp interceptors for auth/logging.
keywords: retrofit http networking okhttp android converter

name: Ktor Client (Multiplatform)
description: Kotlin Multiplatform HTTP client for Android; iOS; and server.
when_to_use: When using KMM and need shared networking layer. Also good for Android-only.
trade_offs: Less mature than Retrofit for Android-specific features; plugin system for serialization.
implementation_notes: Ktor: HttpClient with engine (OkHttp for Android; Darwin for iOS). Kotlinx.serialization. Content negotiation.
keywords: ktor http client multiplatform kotlin networking

name: Alamofire / URLSession (iOS)
description: Networking libraries for iOS — Alamofire wraps URLSession with convenience APIs.
when_to_use: iOS-only projects. URLSession for simple needs; Alamofire for complex interceptors.
trade_offs: Alamofire adds dependency weight; URLSession async/await is sufficient for most cases.
implementation_notes: iOS: URLSession with async/await (modern). Alamofire for retry; intercept; certificate pinning.
keywords: alamofire urlsession networking ios http async

name: Hilt (Android)
description: Android-specific dependency injection built on Dagger with lifecycle integration.
when_to_use: Default DI for Android apps using Jetpack components.
trade_offs: Annotation processing increases build time; Dagger error messages are cryptic.
implementation_notes: Hilt: @HiltAndroidApp + @AndroidEntryPoint + @Inject + @Module + @Provides. Testing: @HiltAndroidTest.
keywords: hilt dagger di injection android module provides

name: Koin (Multiplatform)
description: Lightweight Kotlin DI framework with multiplatform support.
when_to_use: KMM projects or Android apps wanting simpler DI than Hilt.
trade_offs: Runtime resolution (not compile-time) means DI errors appear at runtime; not compile.
implementation_notes: Koin: module { single { } factory { } } + koinApplication. KMM: shared module definitions.
keywords: koin di injection multiplatform kotlin simple

name: Firebase
description: Google's BaaS platform providing auth; database; analytics; and crash reporting for mobile.
when_to_use: When you need rapid backend setup without custom server infrastructure.
trade_offs: Vendor lock-in; pricing scales with usage; limited query flexibility vs custom backend.
implementation_notes: Firebase: Auth + Firestore + Crashlytics + Remote Config + Cloud Messaging (FCM). Both platforms.
keywords: firebase baas auth firestore crashlytics fcm analytics

name: Fastlane
description: Automation tool for building; testing; and deploying iOS and Android apps.
when_to_use: When you need automated release pipeline for both platforms.
trade_offs: Ruby dependency; Fastfile DSL learning curve; some actions are community-maintained.
implementation_notes: Fastlane: scan (test) + gym (build) + deliver/supply (publish) + match (iOS signing).
keywords: fastlane ci cd automate deploy supply deliver match

name: Gradle (Android)
description: Build system for Android projects with Kotlin DSL and dependency management.
when_to_use: All Android projects. AGP 9.1+ and Gradle 9.3+ for modern builds (Meerkat).
trade_offs: Build times can be slow; use configuration cache and build cache.
implementation_notes: Gradle: libs.versions.toml + AGP 9.x. Use kotlin { jvmToolchain(21) } for JDK management. --configuration-cache.
keywords: gradle build android agp 9 gradle 9 meerkat version catalog

name: Xcode + SPM (iOS)
description: Apple's IDE and Swift Package Manager for building and managing iOS projects.
when_to_use: All iOS projects. Xcode 15.3+ preferred.
trade_offs: Xcode-only CI requires macOS runners; SPM resolution can be slow for large dependency graphs.
implementation_notes: Xcode: .xcodeproj or .xcworkspace. SPM: Package.swift. Xcode Cloud for Apple-native CI/CD.
keywords: xcode spm swift package manager build ios

name: Coil (Android)
description: Kotlin-first image loading library with Compose integration for Android.
when_to_use: Default image loading for Jetpack Compose projects. Lightweight alternative to Glide.
trade_offs: Android-only; for KMM consider shared image loading with Ktor + custom caching.
implementation_notes: Coil: AsyncImage (Compose) or ImageRequest. Disk + memory cache. Transformations. SVG/GIF support.
keywords: coil image loading compose android cache async
